title: Fun and rare opportunity
pros: FOH Manager Working the 'Ohana floors at Salesforce is an incredibly unique experience, especially coming from a restaurant background. The benefits and pay are great, but the people are just awesome. No job out there like it!!! Would def recommend.

title: Run run run!
pros: Good perks and benefits- but I guess not so much

title: Not fun
pros: Sales Associate Unorganized management, unclear expectations, and constant pressure create a stressful environment with little support, communication, or growth.

title: Learned a lot but team based experience
pros: Software Engineer Intern San Francisco, CA The culture is good and very open but your experience will really be tied to your teams energy. So make sure that your team is good or else you will have a bad experience

title: Leadership moves goalposts, disrespects your  intelligence, and gaslights you.
pros: Nothing. Don’t believe the inflated reviews about it’s one of the best companies to work for.

title: great benefits

title: Fast-paced & rewarding
pros: Account Executive Really great place to grow your career. Tons of resources, training, fantastic teammates, unmatched parental leave, and great products that are fun to sell. Loved my time here!

title: Deal Strategy
pros: Manager Newly created team with multiple interesting projects. Not a ton of red tape, but also not a lot of structure. Pay is ok, bonuses paid at target and RSUs fair.

title: Typical hustle-culture tech giant, except "Ohana"-style
pros: Senior Technical Writer Remote Can't recommend. I burned out, had to take a medical leave, and when I came back I was quiet-fired. Managers are rewarded for being able to speak on calls well, but they're often incompetent and lacking empathy. If you can survive at dysfunctional family gatherings without once losing your cool, you'd ...

title: Grat growth
pros: Administrator San Diego, CA Perfect place to learn and grow as you work, you never stop learning and hace all the resources in hand, the little time i was there the culture and management were great

title: Overworked, micromanaged
pros: Software Engineer Remote Pay was fair but the micromanagement was absurd. Management gave ridiculous goals yet expected things to be done that was way out of my control and other team dependent. After Salesforce was bought out by VCs, it went downhill.

title: Smart people, rewarding, Innovative
pros: Manager United Kingdom Salesforce is full of smart minds and the passion can be felt in meetings. The teams want to do well and this is felt from CEO down  the chain. Genuinely love working here and hope to do so for many years to come.

title: Demanding work, stretched thin
pros: Architect Do more with less. Extremely competitive to be recognized as a high performer - and difficult to get recognition in general. Devolving quick to an AI slop house that prefers vibe code to quality/reliability.
